channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.description: What the link between the yen and U.S. stocks means for your portfolio.

item.title: Infantino under fire as viral claims drag European Parliament into FIFA row
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Viral claims with millions of views falsely claim. the European Parliament has launched a probe into FIFA chief Gianni Infantino. In reality, the move came from a letter by 72 MEPs to EU football associations, urging them to seek a formal FIFA review into the controversial Balogun red card reversal.

item.title: ‘Naval base will boost security along Taraba waterways’ – Gov Kefas
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Governor Agbu Kefas of Taraba State has said the establishment of a Nigerian Navy base in the state will improve surveillance, deter criminal activities and strengthen security along its waterways. Kefas stated this on Monday while receiving a delegation from the Naval Headquarters, led by

item.title: Israeli strike kills 10-year-old, eight other Palestinians in Gaza
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Nine Palestinians, including a 10-year-old boy and a senior Hamas police officer, were killed on Tuesday in an Israeli strike in Gaza... The post Israeli strike kills 10-year-old, eight other Palestinians in Gaza appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.description: | By: Francis Onyemachi The Nigeria Labour Congress (NLC) has called on the Nigeria Employers’ Consultative Association (NECA) to work with organised labour to develop a governance framework that will ensure artificial intelligence (AI) does not threaten workers’ jobs. The union made the

item.title: ‘How Adeyemi, DG of ‘fake’ PFIPC was arrested in Osun’
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to Ojelabi, Adeyemi was arrested on Tuesday at his hideout in Osun by operatives of the Intelligence Response Team from Abuja.

item.title: Iran declares regional oil export ban amid US strikes

item.title: US military reimposes naval blockade on Iranian ports, launches new strikes
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US forces on Tuesday reimposed a naval blockade to prevent ships from sailing to or from Iran's ports and conducted strikes against the country for a fourth day in response to Tehran's attacks on commercial ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz and on Gulf facilities.

item.title: Foreign Mercenaries Fighting Against Ukraine On Russia’s Behalf
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Russia is recruiting thousands of low-income citizens in African countries, Asia and Cuba and sending them to Ukraine on false pretences to take part in combat operations alongside Russian forces. item.title: Volunteer firefighter admits to starting a fire in the Fontainebleau forest
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Four people are currently in custody in the investigation into the fires ravaging the Fontainebleau forest, south of Paris, including a volunteer firefighter who admitted to the facts, the Fontainebleau prosecutor announced on Tuesday.

item.title: Kano Teacher Docked Over Rape Of Four Sisters
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: An Islamic teacher, Kamal Abdulmumini, could face a death sentence or life imprisonment if convicted of raping four sisters, including a set of twins, in Ungogo Local Government Area of Kano State. Abdulmumini, a tutor at Alhadid Nursery and Primary School, was arraigned before Justice Suleiman Baba

item.title: Trump administration orders ICE to halt traffic stops after Maine shooting, US media reports
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) suspended the use of traffic stops to detain immigrants across the country, US media reported Tuesday, a day after an ICE agent shot and killed a Colombian driver in Maine. The fatal shooting came a week after an ICE officer killed a motorist in Texas, re

item.title: EU bans Sudan gold imports to cut funding for civil war
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The European Union (EU) has banned the purchase, import and transfer of gold from Sudan in a move aimed at cutting off a major source of funding for the country’s ongoing civil war. The decision, approved by EU foreign ministers, also includes a ban on the export of mercury and cyanide to Suda

item.title: Police Arrest ‘Fake Agency’ DG, Adeyemi
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Linus Aleke in Abuja The Nigeria Police Force has apprehended the self-styled ‘Director-General’ of the controversial Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council (PFIPC), Adeniyi Adeyemi, following a bench warrant issued by

item.title: Without Reliable Power, Financial Inclusion Will Stall, Interswitch CEO Warns
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Mitchell Elegbe, founder and group managing director/CEO of Interswitch, has urged Africans to invest massively in energy infrastructure as a critical enabler of financial inclusion and sustainable economic growth across the continent. In a keynote address delivered on his behalf at the 11th edition

item.title: US reimposes blockade on Iranian ports, launches more strikes
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Iran's deputy FM accuses US of destroying the interim peace deal and says Tehran now has no commitments under MoU.

item.title: Osun guber: APC’s Oyebamiji tasks UK, security agencies on peaceful polls
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Governorship candidate of the All Progressives Congress (APC) in Osun State, Bola Oyebamiji, has called for sustained international cooperation to deepen democracy and security in the state ahead of the August 15 poll. Speaking while receiving a delegation from the British High Commission in Osogbo

item.title: Troops arrest two suspected human organ traffickers in Benue
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of Sector 1, Operation Whirl Stroke, OPWS, have arrested two suspected human organ traffickers during an operation in Katsina-Ala Local Government Area of Benue State. Security expert, Zagazola Makama disclosed this in a report he posted on X on Tuesday. Quoting reliable security sources, Mak

item.title: Barca won't give up on Arsenal target Alvarez
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Barcelona are not giving up on signing Arsenal target Julian Alvarez from Atletico Madrid and could raise their offer after the World Cup.

item.title: Report: Super Eagles’ Unity Cup star set to join Middlesbrough
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Super Eagles midfielder Aderemi Adeoye is on the verge of joining English Championship side Middlesbrough from Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL) outfit Ikorodu City, Soccernet.ng reports. ​The 19-year-old defensive midfielder was a standout performer for Ikorodu City, playing a pivotal role in

item.title: My wife’s 2011 Ford Fiesta was totaled. Should we accept a $2,000 insurance settlement or give up the car for $2,700?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “The hood is bent, the radiator is cracked, and the front bumper is destroyed.”

item.title: US President Trump meets with ‘fan of America’ Iraqi PM Ali al-Zaidi
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US President Donald Trump welcomed Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi to the White House.

item.title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: If you’ve been waiting to try Apple’s revamped Siri without installing a developer beta, you now can. The company on Tuesday released the iOS 27 public beta, giving iPhone owners early access to its AI-powered assistant and other new features before the software’s official launch this fall.

item.title: Singer Kulio inaugurated as NANS entertainment director
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS) has inaugurated music artiste, Comrade Chimezie Chibuike Iseoluwa, popularly known as Kulio, as its Director of Entertainment. The post Singer Kulio inaugurated as NANS entertainment director appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.
